---
format: html
title: get_functional_type_classification_path()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_functional_type_classification_path</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get Functional-Type Classification Path</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Finds the latest dated functional-type classification '.qs' file
path for one continent and optional source prefix.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_functional_type_classification_path(
  continent_id,
  data_source_prefix = NULL,
  path_processed = here::here("Data/Processed/Traits")
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="continent_id">continent_id</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single non-empty character string identifying the continent.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_source_prefix">data_source_prefix</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional single non-empty character string identifying a
source-specific FT classification family, such as '&quot;modern&quot;'.
Use 'NULL' for historical paleo/global classification files.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="path_processed">path_processed</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string giving the directory that contains the
FT classification '.qs' files. Defaults to
'here::here(&quot;Data/Processed/Traits&quot;)'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A single character string with the full path to the latest matching
'.qs' file.
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
